name: Institutional Red-Teaming: Deployment Rules, Not Just Models, Causally Shape Multi-Agent AI Safety (2607.07695)
content: We introduce institutional red-teaming, an evaluation methodology for testing deployment rules in multi-agent AI: hold the agents, objectives, and task state fixed, vary only one rule, and attribute the resulting change in collective behavior to that rule. We instantiate the methodology in IABench-CA, a consequence-allocation benchmark spanning 228 contexts, five canonical rules, and seven model populations (33,924 games), with a normative cooperative reference and auto-labelled reasoning traces
summary: 本文提出了一种新的评估方法，用于测试在多智能体AI中的部署规则，并揭示了这些规则对集体安全的影响。
key_insights: 发现部署规则直接改变了集体的安全性
不存在一个默认的安全规则，但选择目标的风险是普遍存在的
身份的突出地位是机制的核心
xihe_relevance: 该内容对曦和AI系统的潜在价值在于多Agent领域中的方法论贡献。
